title: Set sample rate against Nyquist with a guard band
description: Choose a sample rate above twice the highest frequency of interest plus a margin so the anti-alias filter has a realizable transition band.
body: Use when specifying the ADC clock or resampling ratio for a new acquisition path. Do not set the sample rate at exactly twice the maximum frequency, since no real filter rolls off instantly. Actions: identify the highest band of interest, add a transition guard band, and verify the anti-alias filter reaches full attenuation before the Nyquist edge. Validation signals include a swept-tone test showing no folded energy above Nyquist and a filter response that meets stopband attenuation at the fold frequency. Risk boundary: undersampling corrupts data irreversibly, so treat a missing anti-alias stage as a blocking defect and roll back the capture.
